chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P.No.D- 2710 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of MA 3986/2018. 3. For hearing of main case. 03.09.2019. Syed Shafique Ahmed Shah, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional A.G. = The petitioner claims to be a contractor and has filed this petition for recovery of an amount outstanding against official respondents on account of contractual obligations whereby he had supplied different articles after award of the contract. However, after arguing at some length, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he will not press this petition and would avail a proper remedy by filing a Civil Suit for recovery of said amount before the Civil Court but since the limitation period for the same has expired, it would come in way of the petitioner to file the suit. Accordingly, this petition is dismissed as not pressed alongwith listed application. However, if the petitioner files a Civil Suit for recovery of the alleged outstanding amount against the respondents, the limitation period may be considered sympathetically in favour of the petitioner. JUDGE JUDGE Tufail/PA
